mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains in the lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% indicates near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth forecasts downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.0% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE > 7.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 900 thousand units, indicating joint demand weakness across consumer and residential sectors. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 2% AND HOUST < 900 thousand units in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890.0 thousand units near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 99.0 index points, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings downward in response to production weakness.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO < 99.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand net new jobs per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating labor market deterioration. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as the market recognizes recession risk and reprices equity risk premiums upward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100 thousand AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 235 index points while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.0%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative despite inflation. In this regime, equity valuations expand as real asset returns are attractive, producing positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth trajectory shows quarter-over-quarter expansion above 2.5% annualized rate, indicating robust aggregate demand and earnings growth. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in sustained economic expansion and corporate profit growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations during periods of GDPC1 expansion > 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates, creating a stagflationary signal where supply constraints drive prices higher while production capacity weakens. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices equity risk premiums upward in response to stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% in same month
regime_hint: stagflation or supply-driven inflation with production weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
